name: mylua-lsp
description: Lua 5.3+ language support with EmmyLua annotations

darwin.description: macOS override for the mylua-lsp executable path.

linux.description: Linux override for the mylua-lsp executable path.

win32.description: Windows override for the mylua-lsp executable path (include the .exe extension).

mylua.server.path.markdownDescription:
Path to the mylua-lsp executable. Two forms are accepted:

- **string** — one path applied to all platforms (backwards compatible).
- **object** — per-platform overrides keyed by Node.js `process.platform`, e.g.
  ```json
  { "darwin": "/abs/mac/mylua-lsp", "win32": "C:/tools/mylua-lsp.exe" }
  ```

When empty, the extension locates the binary automatically: production builds load `<extension>/server/<bin>`; the Extension Development Host (F5 debug) bypasses `server/` and loads `<extension>/../lsp/target/debug/<bin>` directly so freshly `cargo build`ed changes take effect without re-running `npm run prepackage`.

mylua.debug.fileLog.description: Write debug log to .vscode/mylua-lsp.log in the workspace.

mylua.runtime.version.description: Assumed Lua runtime version. Affects the builtin identifier set used for `undefinedGlobal` suppression and the path of the bundled stdlib stubs auto-loaded via `mylua.workspace.useBundledStdlib`. If a bundled stub tree for the requested version does not exist, the extension falls back to the latest available bundled version (currently 5.4).

mylua.runtime.topKeyword.markdownDescription: Enable top-level keyword splitting in the parser. When `true`, column-0 keywords (`if`, `while`, `for`, `function`, etc.) emit special `TOP_WORD_*` tokens that force block closure — this improves error localization ("error front-loading") but requires nested code to be indented. When `false` (default), all keywords are treated equally regardless of column. Individual files can still override via `---#enable top_keyword` / `---#disable top_keyword` directives.

mylua.require.aliases.markdownDescription: Path aliases for require resolution. The alias is matched as a dot-separated prefix of the module path, and the longest matching alias wins. E.g. `{ "@": "src", "@utils": "src.utils" }` — `require('@utils.foo')` resolves to `src.utils.foo`.

mylua.workspace.include.description: Glob patterns for files to include in workspace indexing.

mylua.workspace.exclude.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude from workspace indexing.

mylua.workspace.indexMode.description: Multi-root workspace strategy: merge all roots or isolate per root.

mylua.workspace.library.markdownDescription: Additional directories to index alongside the workspace, typically for Lua stdlib stubs or third-party EmmyLua annotation packages. Each entry may be an absolute path, a `~/…` path (expanded against `$HOME`), or a path relative to the first workspace root. Files reached via these roots are indexed read-only: they contribute globals / types to hover, goto, completion, and require resolution, but their own diagnostics are suppressed.

mylua.workspace.useBundledStdlib.markdownDescription: When enabled, the extension auto-injects the bundled Lua stdlib stubs (`<extension>/assets/lua<version>/`) into `#mylua.workspace.library#`, so `print`, `string.format`, `table.concat`, etc. have hover / goto / signature help without any user configuration. Disable only if you want to supply your own complete stdlib annotations via `#mylua.workspace.library#`. The version selected follows `#mylua.runtime.version#`.

mylua.index.cacheMode.description: Index persistence: 'summary' caches DocumentSummary to disk, 'memory' is in-process only.

mylua.diagnostics.undefinedGlobal.description: Severity for undefined global variable diagnostics.

mylua.diagnostics.emmyTypeMismatch.description: Severity when a value doesn't match its Emmy type annotation.

mylua.diagnostics.emmyUnknownField.description: Severity for accessing an unknown field on an Emmy-typed value.

mylua.diagnostics.luaFieldError.description: Severity for high-confidence Lua field errors (e.g. indexing nil).

mylua.diagnostics.luaFieldWarning.description: Severity for conservative Lua field warnings (e.g. unknown field on open table).

mylua.diagnostics.duplicateTableKey.description: Severity for duplicate keys inside a single table constructor (`{ a = 1, a = 2 }`).

mylua.diagnostics.unusedLocal.description: Severity for local variables declared but never read. `_`/`_prefix` names are always skipped.

mylua.diagnostics.argumentCountMismatch.description: Severity when a call passes a different number of arguments than the resolved signature expects. Vararg (`...`) absorbs extras; any `---@overload` matching the count suppresses the diagnostic.

mylua.diagnostics.argumentTypeMismatch.description: Severity when an argument literal type doesn't match the resolved parameter's declared `---@param` type. Unknown-typed arguments are skipped silently.

mylua.diagnostics.returnMismatch.description: Severity when a function's `---@return` annotation doesn't match the actual `return` statements. Walks all nested returns (including inside `if`/`do`/`while`) but stops at inner function boundaries.

enumDescriptions: Diagnose the entire workspace (cold-start seeds all files; cascades to all dependants).
Only diagnose files currently opened in editor tabs; cascades skip closed files.

mylua.gotoDefinition.strategy.description: Multi-candidate strategy: 'auto' jumps if one best, else shows list.

mylua.references.strategy.description: Multi-candidate references: 'best' uses top candidate, 'merge' unions all, 'select' prompts.
